{{Quote|After the first Death Star was destroyed, Lemelisk went into hiding, fearing for his life. When Imperial Intelligence agents tracked him down at his remote retreat on Hefi, he thought he was doomed.|Rebel Alliance historian [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]]|<ref name="GG5, 2nd">[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi'', Second Edition]]</ref>}}
'''Hefi''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE-Hefi" /> located in the [[Hefi system/Legends|Hefi system]]<ref name="Online Companion" /> of the [[Abrion sector/Legends|Abrion sector]],<ref name="CSWE-Hefi" /> within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion<ref name="Atlas" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="CSWE-Hefi" /> Covered by landmasses and [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]], Hefi's surface featured varied climates and was capable of supporting various forms of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] life.<ref name="SWR" />

Hefi once served as a private sanctuary for [[Bevel Lemelisk]],<ref name="CSWE-Hefi" /> the designer of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. Following the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] destruction at the hands of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="GG5, 2nd" /> Lemelisk fled into hiding on Hefi, fearing for his life<ref name="CSWE-Hefi" /> for having failed [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].<ref name="GG5, 1st">''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]''</ref> [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] agents tracked Lemelisk to his [[Bevel Lemelisk's Hefi retreat|remote retreat]],<ref name="GG5, 2nd" /> and the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] later executed him for his incompetence.<ref name="CSWE-Bevel Lemelisk">{{CSWECite|II|250|Lemelisk, Bevel}}</ref> However, later findings suggested that this story was likely apocryphal.<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|"Hefi"}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The planet Hefi was first mentioned in the [[1990]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]'', written by [[Michael Stern]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG5, 1st" /> Hefi later appeared as one of 200 playable worlds in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], which provided the planet's only visual depiction, an image also used to illustrate eleven other planets of similar composition in the game.<ref name="SWR" /> In [[2009]], the reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Hefi [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore also Hefi, in grid square S-15.<ref name="Atlas" />

Though presented as fact in ''Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi'',<ref name="GG5, 1st" /> the 1998 ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' notes that the story of Bevel Lemelisk hiding on Hefi following the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] destruction was "likely apocryphal, based on more recent findings."<ref name="SWE" /> However, after that source, both [[2000]]'s ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''<ref name="Guide 3rd edition">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> and [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' would once more present the story as fact.<ref name="CSWE-Hefi" /> Nevertheless, this article presents both versions of the story, effectively as competing reports.

''Star Wars: Rebellion'' provides [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel [[Time/Legends|times]] between each planet in the game. Hefi's intra-[[Sector/Legends|sector]] hyperspace times and distance from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="SWR" /> the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]],<ref name="CSWE-Coruscant">{{CSWECite|I|146-147|Coruscant}}</ref> according to ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' using the game's fastest [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] are as follows. This chart only includes planets whose placement in the Abrion sector was [[canon]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, excepting Coruscant.<ref name="SWR" />
